001/*
002 * Licensed to the Apache Software Foundation (ASF) under one or more
003 * contributor license agreements.  See the NOTICE file distributed with
004 * this work for additional information regarding copyright ownership.
005 * The ASF licenses this file to You under the Apache License, Version 2.0
006 * (the "License"); you may not use this file except in compliance with
007 * the License.  You may obtain a copy of the License at
008 *
009 *      http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
010 *
011 * Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
012 * distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
013 * WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
014 * See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
015 * limitations under the License.
016 */
017package org.apache.juneau.http.header;
018
019import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.AssertionUtils.*;
020import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.ThrowableUtils.*;
021
022import java.security.*;
023import java.util.*;
024import java.util.function.*;
025
026/**
027 * Represents an HTTP <c>Content-Security-Policy</c> (or <c>Content-Security-Policy-Report-Only</c>) response header.
028 *
029 * <p>
030 * A Content Security Policy lets a server declare which content sources a browser may load and execute for a page,
031 * mitigating cross-site-scripting (XSS) and data-injection attacks.  This bean carries an already-rendered policy
032 * value; use {@link Builder} to assemble the value from typed directives.
033 *
034 * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
035 * <p class='bjava'>
036 *    <jc>// Build a static policy and emit it as a response header.</jc>
037 *    ContentSecurityPolicy <jv>csp</jv> = ContentSecurityPolicy.<jsm>create</jsm>()
038 *       .defaultSrc(ContentSecurityPolicy.Builder.<jsf>SELF</jsf>)
039 *       .objectSrc(ContentSecurityPolicy.Builder.<jsf>NONE</jsf>)
040 *       .toHeader();
041 *
042 *    <jc>// =&gt; "Content-Security-Policy: default-src 'self'; object-src 'none'"</jc>
043 * </p>
044 *
045 * <p>
046 * Because this is an {@link HttpStringHeader}, an instance can be added directly to the REST server's
047 * <c>defaultResponseHeaders</c> {@link HttpHeaderList} (e.g. via a <c><ja>@Bean</ja>(name=<js>"defaultResponseHeaders"</js>)</c>
048 * factory method) and is emitted through the normal response-header path.
049 *
050 * <p>
051 * <b>Beta — API subject to change:</b> This type is part of the next-generation REST client and HTTP stack
052 * ({@code org.apache.juneau.marshall.ng.*}).
053 * It is not API-frozen: binary- and source-incompatible changes may appear in the <b>next major</b> Juneau release
054 * (and possibly earlier).
055 *
056 * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
057 *    <li class='jc'>{@link Builder}
058 *    <li class='extlink'><a class='doclink' href='https://www.w3.org/TR/CSP3/'>Content Security Policy Level 3 (W3C WD)</a>
059 *    <li class='extlink'><a class='doclink' href='https://www.w3.org/TR/CSP2/'>Content Security Policy Level 2 (W3C Rec)</a>
060 * </ul>
061 *
062 * @since 10.0.0
063 */
064@SuppressWarnings({
065   "java:S115" // NAME / REPORT_ONLY_NAME mirror the external HTTP header literal exactly.
066})
067public class ContentSecurityPolicy extends HttpStringHeader {
068
069   /** The enforcing header name: {@value}. */
070   public static final String NAME = "Content-Security-Policy";
071
072   /** The observe-only header name: {@value}. */
073   public static final String REPORT_ONLY_NAME = "Content-Security-Policy-Report-Only";
074
075   /**
076    * Constructor with an eager value (enforcing {@value #NAME} header).
077    *
078    * @param value The header value. May be <jk>null</jk>.
079    */
080   public ContentSecurityPolicy(String value) {
081      super(NAME, value);
082   }
083
084   /**
085    * Constructor with a lazy value supplier (enforcing {@value #NAME} header).
086    *
087    * @param valueSupplier Supplier for the header value. Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
088    */
089   public ContentSecurityPolicy(Supplier<String> valueSupplier) {
090      super(NAME, valueSupplier);
091   }
092
093   /**
094    * Constructor allowing the header name to be chosen (enforcing vs. report-only).
095    *
096    * @param name The header name. Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
097    * @param value The header value. May be <jk>null</jk>.
098    */
099   protected ContentSecurityPolicy(String name, String value) {
100      super(name, value);
101   }
102
103   /**
104    * Constructor allowing the header name to be chosen, with a lazy value supplier.
105    *
106    * @param name The header name. Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
107    * @param valueSupplier Supplier for the header value. Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
108    */
109   protected ContentSecurityPolicy(String name, Supplier<String> valueSupplier) {
110      super(name, valueSupplier);
111   }
112
113   /**
114    * Static factory method for an enforcing ({@value #NAME}) header with an eager value.
115    *
116    * @param value The header value. May be <jk>null</jk>.
117    * @return A new instance. Never <jk>null</jk>.
118    */
119   public static ContentSecurityPolicy of(String value) {
120      return new ContentSecurityPolicy(value);
121   }
122
123   /**
124    * Static factory method for an enforcing ({@value #NAME}) header with a lazy value supplier.
125    *
126    * @param valueSupplier Supplier for the header value. Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
127    * @return A new instance. Never <jk>null</jk>.
128    */
129   public static ContentSecurityPolicy of(Supplier<String> valueSupplier) {
130      return new ContentSecurityPolicy(valueSupplier);
131   }
132
133   /**
134    * Static factory method for an observe-only ({@value #REPORT_ONLY_NAME}) header with an eager value.
135    *
136    * <p>
137    * Browsers report violations against this header but do not block anything, letting operators tune a policy
138    * before enforcing it.
139    *
140    * @param value The header value. May be <jk>null</jk>.
141    * @return A new instance. Never <jk>null</jk>.
142    */
143   public static ContentSecurityPolicy ofReportOnly(String value) {
144      return new ContentSecurityPolicy(REPORT_ONLY_NAME, value);
145   }
146
147   /**
148    * Static factory method for an observe-only ({@value #REPORT_ONLY_NAME}) header with a lazy value supplier.
149    *
150    * @param valueSupplier Supplier for the header value. Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
151    * @return A new instance. Never <jk>null</jk>.
152    */
153   public static ContentSecurityPolicy ofReportOnly(Supplier<String> valueSupplier) {
154      return new ContentSecurityPolicy(REPORT_ONLY_NAME, valueSupplier);
155   }
156
157   /**
158    * Creates a new empty {@link Builder}.
159    *
160    * @return A new builder. Never <jk>null</jk>.
161    */
162   public static Builder create() {
163      return Builder.create();
164   }
165
166   /**
167    * Creates a {@link Builder} pre-populated with the nonce-based <i>strict starter</i> preset.
168    *
169    * @return A new builder pre-populated with the strict-starter directives. Never <jk>null</jk>.
170    * @see Builder#strictStarter()
171    */
172   public static Builder strictStarter() {
173      return Builder.strictStarter();
174   }
175
176   //------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
177   // Inner types
178   //------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
179
180   /**
181    * Fluent builder for assembling a <c>Content-Security-Policy</c> header value.
182    *
183    * <p>
184    * Composes the directives defined by
185    * <a class='doclink' href='https://www.w3.org/TR/CSP3/'>CSP Level 3</a> into the {@code directive source source; ...}
186    * wire format expected by the {@code Content-Security-Policy} response header.  Typed setters cover the common
187    * directives; {@link #directive(String, String...)} is a generic escape hatch so new directives never require an API
188    * change.  Setting the same directive more than once <b>replaces</b> the previous source list.
189    *
190    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
191    * <p class='bjava'>
192    *    String <jv>value</jv> = ContentSecurityPolicy.<jsm>create</jsm>()
193    *       .defaultSrc(<jsf>SELF</jsf>)
194    *       .imgSrc(<jsf>SELF</jsf>, <js>"data:"</js>)
195    *       .objectSrc(<jsf>NONE</jsf>)
196    *       .build();
197    *    <jc>// =&gt; "default-src 'self'; img-src 'self' data:; object-src 'none'"</jc>
198    * </p>
199    *
200    * <h5 class='section'>Nonces:</h5>
201    * <p>
202    * Source helper {@link #nonce(String)} renders a <c>'nonce-&lt;token&gt;'</c> source expression.  For per-request
203    * nonces (where the actual token is not known until the response is rendered), use {@link #NONCE_PLACEHOLDER} as the
204    * token and have the REST layer rewrite it per-response via {@link #resolveNonce(String, String)}; the same nonce is
205    * then stamped onto the inline <c>&lt;script&gt;</c>/<c>&lt;style&gt;</c> tags the serializer emits.
206    *
207    * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
208    *    <li class='jc'>{@link ContentSecurityPolicy}
209    *    <li class='extlink'><a class='doclink' href='https://www.w3.org/TR/CSP3/'>Content Security Policy Level 3 (W3C WD)</a>
210    * </ul>
211    *
212    * @since 10.0.0
213    */
214   @SuppressWarnings({
215      "java:S115" // Source-keyword constants mirror external CSP literals exactly (e.g. 'self', 'none').
216   })
217   public static class Builder {
218
219      //--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
220      // Source-expression keyword helpers
221      //--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
222
223      /** The <c>'self'</c> source-expression keyword. */
224      public static final String SELF = "'self'";
225
226      /** The <c>'none'</c> source-expression keyword. */
227      public static final String NONE = "'none'";
228
229      /** The <c>'unsafe-inline'</c> source-expression keyword. */
230      public static final String UNSAFE_INLINE = "'unsafe-inline'";
231
232      /** The <c>'unsafe-eval'</c> source-expression keyword. */
233      public static final String UNSAFE_EVAL = "'unsafe-eval'";
234
235      /** The <c>'strict-dynamic'</c> source-expression keyword. */
236      public static final String STRICT_DYNAMIC = "'strict-dynamic'";
237
238      /**
239       * The placeholder token used in nonce sources when the actual per-request nonce is not yet known.
240       *
241       * <p>
242       * {@link #strictStarter()} emits {@code 'nonce-{nonce}'}; the REST layer substitutes the real nonce per-response
243       * via {@link #resolveNonce(String, String)}.
244       */
245      public static final String NONCE_PLACEHOLDER = "{nonce}";
246
247      /**
248       * Renders a <c>'nonce-&lt;token&gt;'</c> source expression.
249       *
250       * @param token The base64 nonce value (or {@link #NONCE_PLACEHOLDER} for deferred substitution). Must not be <jk>null</jk> or blank.
251       * @return The source expression. Never <jk>null</jk>.
252       * @throws IllegalArgumentException If {@code token} is <jk>null</jk> or blank.
253       */
254      public static String nonce(String token) {
255         assertArgNotNull("token", token);
256         if (token.trim().isEmpty())
257            throw illegalArg("nonce token must not be blank");
258         return "'nonce-" + token + "'";
259      }
260
261      /**
262       * Renders a scheme source expression, ensuring a single trailing colon (e.g. <js>"https"</js> =&gt; <c>https:</c>).
263       *
264       * @param scheme The scheme (with or without a trailing colon). Must not be <jk>null</jk> or blank.
265       * @return The scheme source expression. Never <jk>null</jk>.
266       * @throws IllegalArgumentException If {@code scheme} is <jk>null</jk> or blank.
267       */
268      public static String scheme(String scheme) {
269         assertArgNotNull("scheme", scheme);
270         var s = scheme.trim();
271         if (s.isEmpty())
272            throw illegalArg("scheme must not be blank");
273         return s.endsWith(":") ? s : s + ":";
274      }
275
276      /**
277       * Renders a <c>'sha256-&lt;hash&gt;'</c> (or sha384/sha512) source expression.
278       *
279       * @param algorithm One of <js>"sha256"</js>, <js>"sha384"</js>, or <js>"sha512"</js>. Must not be <jk>null</jk> or blank.
280       * @param base64Hash The base64-encoded hash of the inline block. Must not be <jk>null</jk> or blank.
281       * @return The hash source expression. Never <jk>null</jk>.
282       * @throws IllegalArgumentException If either argument is <jk>null</jk> or blank.
283       */
284      public static String hash(String algorithm, String base64Hash) {
285         assertArgNotNull("algorithm", algorithm);
286         assertArgNotNull("base64Hash", base64Hash);
287         if (algorithm.trim().isEmpty())
288            throw illegalArg("hash algorithm must not be blank");
289         if (base64Hash.trim().isEmpty())
290            throw illegalArg("hash value must not be blank");
291         return "'" + algorithm.trim() + "-" + base64Hash.trim() + "'";
292      }
293
294      private static final SecureRandom SECURE_RANDOM = new SecureRandom();
295
296      /**
297       * Mints a fresh, cryptographically-random CSP nonce.
298       *
299       * <p>
300       * Generates 128 bits (16 bytes) from {@link SecureRandom} encoded as an unpadded base64url string.  Each call
301       * returns a new value; a nonce must never be reused across responses.
302       *
303       * @return A new nonce token. Never <jk>null</jk>.
304       */
305      public static String generateNonce() {
306         var bytes = new byte[16];
307         SECURE_RANDOM.nextBytes(bytes);
308         return Base64.getUrlEncoder().withoutPadding().encodeToString(bytes);
309      }
310
311      /**
312       * Replaces every occurrence of {@link #NONCE_PLACEHOLDER} in a rendered policy value with the supplied nonce.
313       *
314       * @param policyValue The rendered policy value (may contain {@link #NONCE_PLACEHOLDER}). May be <jk>null</jk>.
315       * @param nonce The per-request nonce token. Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
316       * @return The policy value with placeholders substituted, or <jk>null</jk> if {@code policyValue} was <jk>null</jk>.
317       * @throws IllegalArgumentException If {@code nonce} is <jk>null</jk>.
318       */
319      public static String resolveNonce(String policyValue, String nonce) {
320         assertArgNotNull("nonce", nonce);
321         if (policyValue == null)
322            return null;
323         return policyValue.replace(NONCE_PLACEHOLDER, nonce);
324      }
325
326      //--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
327      // Instance
328      //--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
329
330      private final Map<String,List<String>> directives = new LinkedHashMap<>();
331      private boolean reportOnly;
332
333      /**
334       * Creates a new empty builder.
335       *
336       * @return A new builder. Never <jk>null</jk>.
337       */
338      public static Builder create() {
339         return new Builder();
340      }
341
342      /**
343       * Creates a builder pre-populated with the nonce-based <i>strict starter</i> preset.
344       *
345       * <p>
346       * The preset is:
347       * <p class='bcode'>
348       *    default-src 'self';
349       *    script-src 'self' 'nonce-{nonce}';
350       *    style-src 'self' 'nonce-{nonce}';
351       *    img-src 'self' data:;
352       *    connect-src 'self';
353       *    object-src 'none';
354       *    base-uri 'self';
355       *    frame-ancestors 'self'
356       * </p>
357       *
358       * <p>
359       * The {@code {nonce}} tokens are {@link #NONCE_PLACEHOLDER}s; for the policy to function on Juneau-served HTML
360       * (which emits inline {@code <script>}/{@code <style>}) the REST layer must mint a per-request nonce, substitute
361       * it via {@link #resolveNonce(String, String)}, and stamp the same value onto the emitted inline tags.
362       *
363       * @return A new pre-populated builder. Never <jk>null</jk>.
364       */
365      public static Builder strictStarter() {
366         return create()
367            .defaultSrc(SELF)
368            .scriptSrc(SELF, nonce(NONCE_PLACEHOLDER))
369            .styleSrc(SELF, nonce(NONCE_PLACEHOLDER))
370            .imgSrc(SELF, "data:")
371            .connectSrc(SELF)
372            .objectSrc(NONE)
373            .baseUri(SELF)
374            .frameAncestors(SELF);
375      }
376
377      /**
378       * Sets the <c>default-src</c> directive.
379       *
380       * @param sources The source expressions. May be empty.
381       * @return This object.
382       */
383      public Builder defaultSrc(String...sources) {
384         return directive("default-src", sources);
385      }
386
387      /**
388       * Sets the <c>script-src</c> directive.
389       *
390       * @param sources The source expressions. May be empty.
391       * @return This object.
392       */
393      public Builder scriptSrc(String...sources) {
394         return directive("script-src", sources);
395      }
396
397      /**
398       * Sets the <c>style-src</c> directive.
399       *
400       * @param sources The source expressions. May be empty.
401       * @return This object.
402       */
403      public Builder styleSrc(String...sources) {
404         return directive("style-src", sources);
405      }
406
407      /**
408       * Sets the <c>img-src</c> directive.
409       *
410       * @param sources The source expressions. May be empty.
411       * @return This object.
412       */
413      public Builder imgSrc(String...sources) {
414         return directive("img-src", sources);
415      }
416
417      /**
418       * Sets the <c>connect-src</c> directive.
419       *
420       * @param sources The source expressions. May be empty.
421       * @return This object.
422       */
423      public Builder connectSrc(String...sources) {
424         return directive("connect-src", sources);
425      }
426
427      /**
428       * Sets the <c>font-src</c> directive.
429       *
430       * @param sources The source expressions. May be empty.
431       * @return This object.
432       */
433      public Builder fontSrc(String...sources) {
434         return directive("font-src", sources);
435      }
436
437      /**
438       * Sets the <c>object-src</c> directive.
439       *
440       * @param sources The source expressions. May be empty.
441       * @return This object.
442       */
443      public Builder objectSrc(String...sources) {
444         return directive("object-src", sources);
445      }
446
447      /**
448       * Sets the <c>base-uri</c> directive.
449       *
450       * @param sources The source expressions. May be empty.
451       * @return This object.
452       */
453      public Builder baseUri(String...sources) {
454         return directive("base-uri", sources);
455      }
456
457      /**
458       * Sets the <c>frame-ancestors</c> directive.
459       *
460       * @param sources The source expressions. May be empty.
461       * @return This object.
462       */
463      public Builder frameAncestors(String...sources) {
464         return directive("frame-ancestors", sources);
465      }
466
467      /**
468       * Sets the <c>report-uri</c> directive (deprecated in CSP3 but still widely supported).
469       *
470       * @param uris The collector URIs. May be empty.
471       * @return This object.
472       */
473      public Builder reportUri(String...uris) {
474         return directive("report-uri", uris);
475      }
476
477      /**
478       * Sets the <c>report-to</c> directive (the CSP3 reporting-group mechanism).
479       *
480       * @param groups The reporting-group names. May be empty.
481       * @return This object.
482       */
483      public Builder reportTo(String...groups) {
484         return directive("report-to", groups);
485      }
486
487      /**
488       * Generic escape hatch for setting any directive by name.
489       *
490       * <p>
491       * Replaces any previously-registered source list for the same directive while preserving its original position.
492       *
493       * @param name The directive name (e.g. <js>"worker-src"</js>). Must not be <jk>null</jk> or blank.
494       * @param sources The source expressions. May be empty (renders the bare directive name).
495       * @return This object.
496       * @throws IllegalArgumentException If {@code name} is <jk>null</jk> or blank.
497       */
498      public Builder directive(String name, String...sources) {
499         assertArgNotNull("name", name);
500         var n = name.trim();
501         if (n.isEmpty())
502            throw illegalArg("directive name must not be blank");
503         directives.put(n, new ArrayList<>(Arrays.asList(sources)));
504         return this;
505      }
506
507      /**
508       * Flips the rendered header to the observe-only {@link ContentSecurityPolicy#REPORT_ONLY_NAME} variant.
509       *
510       * @return This object.
511       */
512      public Builder reportOnly() {
513         return reportOnly(true);
514      }
515
516      /**
517       * Sets whether the rendered header uses the observe-only {@link ContentSecurityPolicy#REPORT_ONLY_NAME} variant.
518       *
519       * @param value <jk>true</jk> to emit {@code Content-Security-Policy-Report-Only} instead of {@code Content-Security-Policy}.
520       * @return This object.
521       */
522      public Builder reportOnly(boolean value) {
523         reportOnly = value;
524         return this;
525      }
526
527      /**
528       * Returns <jk>true</jk> if this builder is configured to emit the report-only header variant.
529       *
530       * @return A boolean flag.
531       */
532      public boolean isReportOnly() {
533         return reportOnly;
534      }
535
536      /**
537       * Builds the rendered policy value (the header value only, not including the header name).
538       *
539       * <p>
540       * Returns an empty string when no directives have been registered.
541       *
542       * @return The policy value. Never <jk>null</jk>.
543       */
544      public String build() {
545         var parts = new ArrayList<String>(directives.size());
546         directives.forEach((name, sources) -> {
547            if (sources.isEmpty())
548               parts.add(name);
549            else
550               parts.add(name + " " + String.join(" ", sources));
551         });
552         return String.join("; ", parts);
553      }
554
555      /**
556       * Builds a {@link ContentSecurityPolicy} header bean from this builder.
557       *
558       * <p>
559       * The header name is {@link ContentSecurityPolicy#REPORT_ONLY_NAME} when {@link #isReportOnly()} is <jk>true</jk>,
560       * otherwise {@link ContentSecurityPolicy#NAME}.
561       *
562       * @return A new header bean carrying {@link #build()}. Never <jk>null</jk>.
563       */
564      public ContentSecurityPolicy toHeader() {
565         return reportOnly ? ContentSecurityPolicy.ofReportOnly(build()) : ContentSecurityPolicy.of(build());
566      }
567
568      @Override
569      public String toString() {
570         return build();
571      }
572   }
573}
